## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** A coupon application feature in TypeScript across three files in `.tmp/coupons/`.

**Approach:** Clean domain-driven design ‚Äî a repository interface isolates data access from business logic. `CouponService` applies coupons through a strict validation chain. Tests use a lightweight inline mock factory (no mocking library required).

| Path | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `.tmp/coupons/coupon-repository.ts` | exists/verified | `Coupon` type + `CouponRepository` interface |
| `.tmp/coupons/coupon-service.ts` | exists/verified | `CouponService` class + `CouponError` with typed codes |
| `.tmp/coupons/coupon-service.test.ts` | exists/verified | Full test suite ‚Äî 1 describe block per AC |

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage

| AC1: SAVE10 ‚Üí cartTotal √ó 0.90 | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC1 ‚Äî valid coupon SAVE10 applies 10% discount` (3 tests) | Also covers $0 edge case and fractional totals |
| AC2: expired ‚Üí throws `COUPON_EXPIRED` | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC2 ‚Äî expired coupon throws COUPON_EXPIRED` (2 tests) | Checks both throw type and `.code` |
| AC3: unknown ‚Üí throws `COUPON_NOT_FOUND` | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC3 ‚Äî unknown coupon throws COUPON_NOT_FOUND` (2 tests) | Empty repo fixture ensures true not-found |
| AC4: min $50, cart $40 ‚Üí throws `ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET` | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC4 ‚Äî minimum order requirement‚Ä¶` (4 tests) | Also asserts boundary: exactly $50 passes |
| AC5: single-use already used ‚Üí throws `COUPON_ALREADY_USED` | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC5 ‚Äî single-use coupon already used‚Ä¶` (3 tests) | Also asserts fresh single-use coupon works |

## Confidence Assessment

- **Level:** 98%
- **High confidence:** All 5 ACs covered, validation order is explicit (`NOT_FOUND ‚Üí EXPIRED ‚Üí MIN_ORDER ‚Üí ALREADY_USED`), edge cases (zero cart, exact boundary, fresh single-use) are tested
- **Low confidence:** Nothing significant
- **Assumptions:** `discountPercent` is a 0‚Äì100 integer (e.g. `10` = 10% off); the repository is read-only and does not mark coupons as used after application (persistence is out of scope per spec)
